answer:
\boxed{C}

To design the bar, calculate the required cross-sectional area as \( \frac{4000 \text{ lb} \times 2.5}{175 \text{ psi}} \approx 57.14 \text{ in}^2 \). The largest cross-sectional area among the options is **18 in. × 6 in.**, which equals **108 in²**, safely exceeding the requirement. This ensures the bar meets the tension load with the specified factor of safety.
